title: Race-lap ranking specialist with monotonic post-calibration
summary: AUC is a ranking metric, and CatBoost supports groupwise/pairwise ranking objectives.
rationale: AUC is a ranking metric, and CatBoost supports groupwise/pairwise ranking objectives. In this problem, cars on the same race lap form a natural decision set, so a ranking model can learn relative pit urgency within a lap cohort; then a monotonic calibrator can convert that rank score into usable probabilities without harming ordering.
implementation_hint: Create groups by (Year,Race,LapNumber) or a slightly broader race-local window; train a CatBoost ranking model such as YetiRank/PairLogit on within-group stop ordering, then stack its out-of-fold scores with the binary classifier. Apply beta or isotonic calibration on race-held-out OOF predictions only; monotonic calibration preserves ranking while improving probability quality.
expected_effect: Should add complementary signal in crowded strategic moments where only a few cars are realistic pit candidates.
risk: Group construction is delicate: too narrow yields tiny groups, too broad mixes unrelated contexts; ranking scores may help LB only if calibration is done leak-safely.
